name: Views of the Castle by the Guadiana River — circular from Medellín
name_original: Vistas del Castillo por el Río Guadiana — circular desde Medellín
region_intro: Jogging around Medellín offers diverse landscapes within the Aburrá Valley, surrounded by the Andes Mountains. The city's natural setting provides varied terrain, from urban green spaces along the Medellín River to challenging inclines on surrounding hills. Runners can find routes that traverse lush forests and offer panoramic city views, blending urban and natural environments. This unique geography makes Medellín a suitable destination for various running experiences. Best jogging routes around Medellín The most popular jogging route is Silo de Santa Amalia by La Morra, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes about 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route features gentle elevation changes suitable for a steady run. Another top favourite among local runners is Views of the Castle by the Guadiana River — circular from Medellín, a moderate 8.2 miles (13.3 km) path. This route offers scenic views of the castle and follows the Guadiana River. Local runners also love the Castillo de Medellín — circular por las Vegas Altas del Guadiana, a 2.0 miles (3.2 km) trail leading through the plains of the Guadiana, often completed in about 20 minutes. Jogging around Medellín is defined by its hilly terrain, river corridors, and accessible green spaces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy urban loops to more challenging mountain ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 250 runners have used komoot to explore Medellín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Medellín?
A: Komoot offers nearly 50 diverse running routes around Medellín. These range from easy urban loops to more challenging mountain ascents, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Medellín?
A: Medellín's running trails offer a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find routes with challenging inclines in the surrounding Andes Mountains, as well as flatter paths along the Medellín River and within urban green spaces like Parques del Río. The city's unique geography provides options from lush forests to scenic city views.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Medellín?
A: Yes, Medellín has several running routes suitable for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280334767/castillo-de-medellin-circular-por-las-vegas-altas-del-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Medellín — circular por las Vegas Altas del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.0-mile (3.2 km) trail that takes about 20 minutes to complete, offering a gentle introduction to running in the region.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Medellín?
A: Yes, many running routes in Medellín are circular, offering convenient loops. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282803562/views-of-the-castle-by-the-guadiana-river-circular-from-medellin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Castle by the Guadiana River — circular from Medellín&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.2-mile (13.3 km) moderate path that provides scenic views and follows the river.

Q: What are some scenic running trails with good viewpoints in Medellín?
A: For breathtaking views, consider routes that ascend the surrounding hills. Cerro de Las Tres Cruces is a popular spot for locals, offering panoramic views of the city. While not a specific komoot route in this guide, the general area provides challenging runs with rewarding vistas. You can also find routes that offer views of landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medellín Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running routes near natural features like rivers or waterfalls in Medellín?
A: Absolutely. The Medellín River corridor, particularly areas like Parques del Río, offers extensive running paths alongside the water. For more natural settings, the surrounding mountains feature lush forests and even waterfalls like Salto del Ángel, providing scenic backdrops for your runs. While specific komoot routes in this guide focus on the river and plains, the region is rich with such natural beauty.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions I can see while running in Medellín?
A: Yes, several routes offer glimpses of historical landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282803562/views-of-the-castle-by-the-guadiana-river-circular-from-medellin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Castle by the Guadiana River — circular from Medellín&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic views of the castle. You can also explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5725452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Theater and Castle of Medellín&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medellín Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which are significant historical sites in the region.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Medellín?
A: Medellín is known as the 'City of Eternal Spring' due to its year-round mild climate, making it an excellent destination for running at any time. The consistent pleasant temperatures mean you can enjoy the trails comfortably throughout the year, without extreme heat or cold.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, Medellín offers more difficult routes. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284550272/from-mengabril-to-the-condado-de-medellin-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Mengabril to the Condado de Medellín — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 15.0 km (9.3 miles) route with notable elevation changes, perfect for a rigorous workout.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Medellín?
A: The komoot community highly values the diverse landscapes and varied terrain for jogging in Medellín. Runners often praise the blend of urban green spaces and accessible natural environments, as well as the challenging inclines that provide excellent workouts. Over 250 runners have explored Medellín's trails with komoot, appreciating the unique geographical setting.

Q: Are there any urban green spaces ideal for a relaxed run within Medellín?
A: Yes, Medellín boasts several urban green spaces perfect for relaxed runs. Jardín Botánico offers tranquil trails amidst diverse flora, while Parques del Río Medellín provides ample open space and running paths along the river. Primer Parque de Laureles is another refreshing park with tall trees, ideal for a peaceful jog.

Q: Can I find running routes that are less crowded in Medellín?
A: While popular spots like Cerro de Las Tres Cruces can be busy, you can find less crowded options. Exploring routes in Parque Arví, accessible via Metrocable, offers extensive trails in a vast ecological park, providing a more serene running experience away from the city bustle. El Salado Ecological Park, just outside the city, also offers shaded trails for a quieter run.
tips: We are at the foot of the Castillo del Condado de Medellín, rolling along a wide dirt road near the beautiful 17th century bridge in Medellín. At the foot of the castle is the Roman theater and the church of Santiago Apóstol. The visit is highly recommended (€2 adults)
From this path that passes by the riverbed of the Río Guadiana we find beautiful views of the riverbank and of this bridge to the north of the town that helps to cross the river itself.
